file_insights.description: Replaced direct fs::write with atomic temp+rename pattern for providers file persistence
file_insights.impact: Prevents partial writes and ensures providers.json is either fully updated or untouched

patterns_discovered.implementation: Create temporary file with .tmp extension, write content, then rename to target path
patterns_discovered.benefit: Guarantees atomic writes even on filesystems without true atomic operations

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Temp file cleanup on error paths
gotchas_discovered.note: Current implementation does not explicitly clean up .tmp file if fs::rename fails
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Rust's Result type will drop the written file, but explicit cleanup pattern may be needed

gotchas_discovered.note: fs::rename may not be atomic across different filesystems; ensure providers.json target is on same fs as temp location
gotchas_discovered.context: Project memory notes stale targets after fs::rename; this pattern mitigates but doesn't eliminate risk

approach_outcome.description: Successfully implemented atomic write pattern for providers file using temp file + rename

recommendations.recommendation: Extract atomic write pattern into reusable helper function
recommendations.rationale: Prevents duplication and ensures consistency across all critical file writes in bop codebase

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit temp file cleanup on error
recommendations.rationale: Ensures .tmp files don't accumulate if operations fail; consider using ScopedTempFile or defer-cleanup pattern
recommendations.implementation: Wrap in custom Result handler or use try_scope pattern

recommendations.recommendation: Apply same pattern to other state mutations in dispatcher
recommendations.rationale: Project memory indicates state machine relies on atomic fs operations; other critical paths may need similar hardening
recommendations.scope: Review all fs::write calls in merge-gate and dispatcher code paths

recommendations.recommendation: Document atomic write guarantees in code comments
recommendations.rationale: Future maintainers should understand why temp+rename is used vs simpler fs::write
recommendations.note: Especially relevant given project's reliance on atomic filesystem operations for card state
